response:
Think of a restaurant:

  • You sit at a table and look at the menu.  
  • You tell the waiter what you want.  
  • The waiter takes your order to the kitchen, then brings your food back.  
  • You never need to know how the kitchen works—you just get what you asked for.

An API works the same way for programs or apps. One program says, “I’d like this,” the API carries that message to another program, and then brings back the answer. You get what you need without ever seeing all the behind-the-scenes steps.
